shared_properties: Backdrop keeps a 16x16 px masonry rhythm and low-saturation dungeon palette tuned for a 320x180 internal render scaled to 1280x720 output.
The center shaft stays darker than the wall edges so the falling route stays readable during fast descent.
differences: This asset delivers a tall looping shaft backdrop with a separate dust band region prepared inside the same sheet for parallax slicing.

reference_images.label: Slip Down moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Dungeon depth, cold lighting, and stone surface mood grounding.

reference_images.label: Slip Down GDD mirror
reference_images.purpose: Backdrop parallax speed, camera rules, and stage timeline grounding.

reference_images.label: Slip Down S3 GDD
reference_images.purpose: Primary source for palette, resolution, and vertical descent tone.

bevy_constraints.bundle_strategy: Use layered SpriteBundle instances for the stone shaft and dust strip, with a ParallaxLayer marker controlling the 0.4x scroll rate.
bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load world/dungeon_descent_backdrop.png through AssetServer during Loading and keep the handle in SlipDownArtHandles keyed by asset_key.
runtime_notes: Backdrop width is authored for 1280 px output while preserving 16 px tile cadence.
Dust strip slices scroll at 0.4x camera speed and never move upward.
Keep the central fall lane visually open for obstacle silhouettes.

context.role: Primary vertical shaft background visible through the full descent loop.
context.usage: Visible from the opening room through the exit gate sequence with camera movement restricted to downward travel.

deliverables.notes: Single tall backdrop sheet with a lower-contrast dust region for parallax slicing.

acceptance_criteria: Backdrop file size is exactly 1280x1440 px and preserves a readable central lane across the full height.
Stone walls use only the declared dungeon palette and keep 16x16 px block cadence with no anti-aliased edges.
Dust strip region supports 0.4x parallax motion without exposing a visible seam during one full screen-height loop.
